summary: The first_proof_2 dataset contains 10 research-level problems, of which 6 (prob-01 through prob-05 and prob-07) have a resolved proof issue each, indicating strong traction. No issues remain open across the tracked problems, while a separate proof-status layer shows uniform 100-issue activity per question (q1-q10), suggesting heavy review or verification workload that warrants attention.
problems: Proof issues exist only for 6 of 10 problems (prob-01 to 05, prob-07); prob-06, prob-08, prob-09, and prob-10 have no tracked proof issues, leaving coverage gaps.
The proof-status layer reports a uniform 100 issues for every question q1-q10, an unusual flat distribution that may indicate auto-generated, unfiltered, or unprocessed verification items.
No open issues are recorded for any problem, which could mask unverified or unreviewed proofs rather than confirm completion.
Disconnect between the per-problem issue list (6 problems with resolved proofs) and the proof-status counts (all 10 active) makes true progress hard to assess.
highlights: Six problems (prob-01, 02, 03, 04, 05, 07) already have resolved proof issues, showing solid forward momentum.
Zero open issues across all tracked problems indicates a clean, well-managed issue queue.
Coverage spans diverse fields (descriptive set theory, PL geometry, probability, Riemannian geometry), demonstrating broad benchmark engagement.
Problems prob-04 (k-dilation inequalities) and prob-07 (quasi-reduced words) are resolved, suggesting reproducible proof workflows for similar combinatorial/geometric problems.

suggested_todos.title: Audit prob-06, prob-08, prob-09, prob-10 to create or surface proof issues and close the coverage gap
Reconcile the uniform 100-issue proof-status counts (q1-q10) with the per-problem resolved-issue list to clarify real progress
Verify that the 6 'resolved' proofs have passed peer/formal review rather than being marked done prematurely
Prioritize prob-06 next, leveraging proof patterns from the resolved geometry/probability problems for quick wins
Document the workflow that produced resolutions for prob-04 and prob-07 to replicate on remaining open problems
